cells.text: To evaluate the safety and reactogenicity of [PROTOCOL_A012] vaccine vs. placebo.

Frequency of solicited local and systemic adverse events (AEs) through 7-days after each vaccination. Frequency of unsolicited AEs through 28 days after each vaccination. Frequency of SAEs, medically-attended AEs, and AESIs throughout the entire study period.
To describe the immune response to [PROTOCOL_A012] at baseline, 1 month after each vaccination (Month 1, Month 3, and Month 7), and 4 months after the second vaccination (Month 6).

Geometric mean titer (GMT) of serum neutralizing antibody (nAb) against epithelial cell infection at Day 1, Month 1, Month 3, Month 6, Month 7, and Month 10. GMT of serum nAb against fibroblast infection at Day 1, Month 1, Month 3, Month 6, Month 7, and Month 10. GMR of serum nAb titers against epithelial cell infection at Month 1, Month 3, Month 6, Month 7, and Month 10 over baseline (Day 1). GMR of serum nAb titers against fibroblast cell infection at Month 1, Month 3, Month 6, Month 7, and Month 10 over baseline (Day 1). GMT of antigen-specific antibody responses against gB and Pentamer as measured by enzyme-linked immunosorbent assay (ELISA) at Day 1, Month 1, Month 3, Month 6, Month 7, and Month 10. Proportion of subjects with a 4-fold increase in serum nAb against epithelial cell infection at Month 1, Month 3, Month 7 and Month 10 compared to Day 1. Proportion of subjects with a 4-fold increase in serum nAb against fibroblast infection at Month 1, Month 3, Month 7 and Month 10 compared to Day 1.
To evaluate the ability of [PROTOCOL_A012] to prevent primary CMV from 1 month after the 3rd vaccination.

At Visit Month 7 and thereafter, demonstration of serologic and virologic evidence of primary CMV infection, as defined by: 1) Demonstration of a 4-fold increase over baseline in serum [INVESTIGATIONAL_PRODUCT_A] to one or more CMV proteins not encoded by [PROTOCOL_A012] (pp150, pp28, pp52, pp38) and 2) Demonstration of CMV by PCR in at least one urine, blood, or saliva sample at seroconversion

or thereafter. Immunoblot analysis may be utilized to confirm primary infection if CMV is not detected. Subjects experiencing symptoms consistent with primary CMV infection (fever, rash, cervical lymphadenopathy) will be asked to present to the research site for further evaluation.
To describe the durability of the immune response to [PROTOCOL_A012] at 12 months and 24 months after the last vaccination (Month 18 and Month 30).

GMT of serum nAb against epithelial cell infection at Month 18 and end of study (Month 30). GMT of serum nAb against fibroblast infection at Month 18 and end of study (Month 30). GMR of serum nAb titers against epithelial cell infection at Month 18 and Month 30 over baseline (Day 1). GMR of serum nAb titers against fibroblast cell infection at Month 18 and Month 30 over baseline over baseline (Day 1). GMT of antigen-specific antibody responses against gB and Pentamer as measured by ELISA at Month 18 and end of study (Month 30). Proportion of subjects with a 4-fold increase in serum neutralizing antibodies against epithelial cell infection at Month 18 and Month 30 (12 and 24 months after the third vaccination) compared to Day 1. Proportion of subjects with a 4-fold increase in serum neutralizing antibodies against fibroblast infection at Month 18 and Month 30 (12 and 24 months after the third vaccination) compared to Day 1. Reverse cumulative distribution of serum neutralizing antibody titers against epithelial cell infection and fibroblast infection at Day 1, Month 1, Month 3, Month 7, Month 10, ,Month 18, and end of study (Month 30).

In subjects meeting the secondary endpoint of primary CMV infection, to compare duration and quantity of viral shedding in urine, blood, and saliva in subjects receiving [PROTOCOL_A012] vaccine vs. placebo.
Quantitative CMV PCR of saliva, blood, and urine collected through the end of the study.

Screening Procedures • Informed consent • Blood draw: CMV [INVESTIGATIONAL_PRODUCT_A]/IgM, CBC, AST, ALT, HIV 1/2 antibody • Urine pregnancy test • Inclusion/exclusion criteria • Medical history/Con Meds
Day 1 Procedures • Demographic data • Baseline vital signs and physical exam • Randomization

Day 1, Month 2, and Month 6 Vaccination Procedures • Confirm eligibility • Urine pregnancy test • Administer vaccination • 60-minute post-vaccination assessment • Training on Diary Card completion and distribution of Diary Card

cells.text: Sample Collection for CMV PCR: Clinic visits • Saliva sample • Urine sample • Blood sample
Sample Collection for CMV PCR: Mail-in urine samples
Return and review of Diary Card
Assessment / Collection of SAEs & AEs of Special Interest (AESI)
